page.title: Astondoa 40 vs Sunseeker Manhattan 66: Which Yacht Suits Your Marbella Charter?

data.meta_description: Compare Astondoa 40 vs Sunseeker Manhattan 66 for your Marbella charter. See price, capacity, and features to decide which yacht suits your day on the Costa del Sol.
data.summary: A detailed comparison of the Astondoa 40 and Sunseeker Manhattan 66 for Marbella boat charters, covering price, capacity, and onboard experience to help you choose the right yacht.
key_facts: Sunseeker Manhattan 66: not in our fleet; pricing on request.
Astondoa 40 departs from Puerto Banús with skipper, drinks, and snacks included.
Sunseeker Manhattan 66 typically accommodates 10-12 guests and costs €4,000–€6,000/day.
Both yachts offer flybridge seating, sun pads, and a saloon with air conditioning.
Astondoa 40 is ideal for small groups; Manhattan 66 suits larger parties or corporate events.
All our charters include fuel, insurance, and Spanish IVA (21%).
Book the Astondoa 40 directly with us for a fixed price; Manhattan 66 requires a custom quote.

faq.q: What is the main difference between Astondoa 40 and Sunseeker Manhattan 66?
faq.a: The Astondoa 40 is a 12.5-metre sport cruiser for up to 9 guests, costing €749 for 2 hours. The Sunseeker Manhattan 66 is a 20-metre flybridge motor yacht for up to 12 guests, with daily rates from €4,000. The Astondoa is more affordable and agile; the Manhattan offers more space and luxury.

faq.q: Which yacht is better for a small group in Marbella?
faq.a: The Astondoa 40 is perfect for groups of 6–9. It's compact, easy to handle, and includes all drinks and snacks. The Sunseeker Manhattan 66 would be oversized for a small group and costs significantly more.

faq.q: Can I charter the Sunseeker Manhattan 66 through Boat Rental Marbella?
faq.a: No, we do not have the Sunseeker Manhattan 66 in our fleet. We offer the Astondoa 40 and other boats like the Azimut 39 or Mangusta 80. For large groups, consider our Canados 86 or Ferretti 94.

faq.q: What is included in the Astondoa 40 charter price?
faq.a: The Astondoa 40 charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden costs.

faq.q: How many people can the Astondoa 40 accommodate?
faq.a: The Astondoa 40 has a maximum capacity of 9 passengers. This includes all guests, but not the skipper. For larger groups, we recommend the Azimut 58 Flybridge (12 pax) or Mangusta 80 (12 pax).

faq.q: Is the Sunseeker Manhattan 66 available for charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Yes, several operators in Puerto Banús and Marbella Marina offer the Sunseeker Manhattan 66 for charter. Prices start around €4,000 per day and vary by season and duration. Contact us for a custom quote.

faq.q: Which yacht has better fuel efficiency?
faq.a: The Astondoa 40 is more fuel-efficient due to its smaller size and lighter weight. The Sunseeker Manhattan 66 consumes more fuel, but fuel is included in our Astondoa charter price, whereas Manhattan charters often add fuel surcharges.

faq.q: Can I book the Astondoa 40 for a full day in Marbella?
faq.a: Yes, the Astondoa 40 is available for 8-hour charters at €2,299. You can cruise to Cabopino, Cala del Faro, or Estepona. All-inclusive pricing means no extra costs for drinks or snacks.

data.body_html:
Choosing between the Astondoa 40 and the Sunseeker Manhattan 66 for your Marbella charter comes down to budget, group size, and the experience you want. The Astondoa 40 is a sleek 12.5-metre sport cruiser from our fleet, perfect for intimate days at sea. The Sunseeker Manhattan 66 is a larger flybridge motor yacht often chartered from other operators. This comparison covers price, capacity, features, and local cruising options so you can decide which yacht suits your day on the Costa del Sol.

Astondoa 40 vs Sunseeker Manhattan 66: Key Specs at a Glance

Here’s a quick head-to-head of the main specifications for both yachts when chartered in Marbella.

FeatureAstondoa 40Sunseeker Manhattan 66
Length12.5 m~20 m
Max Guests9 pax12 pax
Starting Price (2h)€749~€4,000/day
Full Day (8h)€2,299~€4,000–€6,000
InclusionsSkipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, IVAOften skipper + fuel extra
Departure PortPuerto BanúsPuerto Banús / Marbella
FlybridgeYesYes

The Astondoa 40 offers transparent all-inclusive pricing, while the Manhattan 66 typically has variable rates and may add fuel surcharges.

Price and Value: Astondoa 40 vs Sunseeker Manhattan 66

The Astondoa 40 is our Tier A boat, priced at €749 for 2 hours and €2,299 for 8 hours. That includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, and 21% IVA. No hidden extras.

The Sunseeker Manhattan 66, not in our fleet, typically costs €4,000–€6,000 per day from other operators. Fuel, skipper gratuity, and catering are often separate. For a budget-friendly option with predictable costs, the Astondoa 40 wins.

Capacity and Comfort: Which Yacht Fits Your Group?

With a maximum of 9 guests, the Astondoa 40 is ideal for couples, small families, or a group of friends. It has a compact flybridge, a small saloon, and a forward sun pad.

The Sunseeker Manhattan 66 accommodates up to 12 guests across three cabins, a larger saloon, and a spacious flybridge. If you’re planning a corporate event or a larger party, the Manhattan offers more room. For groups of 8–9, the Astondoa 40 provides an intimate, cost-effective experience.

Onboard Experience: Cruising from Puerto Banús

Both yachts depart from Puerto Banús, the glamorous marina in Marbella. With the Astondoa 40, you can cruise to Cala del Faro for swimming, anchor near Cabopino, or head west to Estepona. The boat is nimble and easy to handle, allowing you to explore secluded coves.

The Sunseeker Manhattan 66 offers a more stable ride in bigger seas and has a larger flybridge for sunbathing. It’s better suited for longer trips to Sotogrande or the Gibraltar Strait. However, its size limits access to smaller anchorages.

Explore all our yachts on the Boat Rental Marbella homepage.

Which Yacht Should You Choose?

Choose the Astondoa 40 if:

  • You have 6–9 guests.
  • You want a fixed, all-inclusive price with no surprises.
  • You prefer a sporty, agile boat for short trips or half-day charters.
  • You value included drinks and snacks.

Choose the Sunseeker Manhattan 66 if:

  • You have 10–12 guests and need more cabin space.
  • Budget is flexible and you want a larger flybridge.
  • You’re planning an all-day or multi-day itinerary.

For a direct booking with guaranteed inclusions, the Astondoa 40 is the smarter choice. See our Astondoa 40 charter guide for more details.

Alternatives in Our Fleet

If you like the Astondoa 40 but want more capacity, consider the Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax) or the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax, includes a jet ski). Both depart from Puerto Banús and offer all-inclusive pricing.

For a smaller, faster option, the Pershing 46 (14m, 10 pax) is another sporty choice. Browse our full fleet on the Boat Rental Marbella homepage.

Frequently asked questions

What is the main difference between Astondoa 40 and Sunseeker Manhattan 66?

The Astondoa 40 is a 12.5-metre sport cruiser for up to 9 guests, costing €749 for 2 hours. The Sunseeker Manhattan 66 is a 20-metre flybridge motor yacht for up to 12 guests, with daily rates from €4,000. The Astondoa is more affordable and agile; the Manhattan offers more space and luxury.

Which yacht is better for a small group in Marbella?

The Astondoa 40 is perfect for groups of 6–9. It's compact, easy to handle, and includes all drinks and snacks. The Sunseeker Manhattan 66 would be oversized for a small group and costs significantly more.

Can I charter the Sunseeker Manhattan 66 through Boat Rental Marbella?

No, we do not have the Sunseeker Manhattan 66 in our fleet. We offer the Astondoa 40 and other boats like the Azimut 39 or Mangusta 80. For large groups, consider our Canados 86 or Ferretti 94.

What is included in the Astondoa 40 charter price?

The Astondoa 40 charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). No hidden costs.

How many people can the Astondoa 40 accommodate?

The Astondoa 40 has a maximum capacity of 9 passengers. This includes all guests, but not the skipper. For larger groups, we recommend the Azimut 58 Flybridge (12 pax) or Mangusta 80 (12 pax).

Is the Sunseeker Manhattan 66 available for charter in Marbella?

Yes, several operators in Puerto Banús and Marbella Marina offer the Sunseeker Manhattan 66 for charter. Prices start around €4,000 per day and vary by season and duration. Contact us for a custom quote.

Which yacht has better fuel efficiency?

The Astondoa 40 is more fuel-efficient due to its smaller size and lighter weight. The Sunseeker Manhattan 66 consumes more fuel, but fuel is included in our Astondoa charter price, whereas Manhattan charters often add fuel surcharges.

Can I book the Astondoa 40 for a full day in Marbella?

Yes, the Astondoa 40 is available for 8-hour charters at €2,299. You can cruise to Cabopino, Cala del Faro, or Estepona. All-inclusive pricing means no extra costs for drinks or snacks.
